content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 K Street
Sacramento 95814
() 322-5660


                                        October 3, 1975


                                                            75 - 170

Mr. Bill Green, Jr.
Senior Consultant
Senate Republican Caucus
1116 Ninth Street, Suite 68
Sacramento, California 95814

                            Re:  Request for Opinion
                                 No. 75-179

Dear Mr. Green:

     Thank you for your letter of September 18,
1975, requesting an opinion with regard to reporting
requirements under the Political Reform Act. Since
your letter raises no substantial question involving
an interpretation of the Act, your request for a
formal opinion is denied.

     Your car-pooling arrangement does not consti-
tute either a gift or a source of income since you
and your lobbyist friend share expenses equally.
Therefore, the Act does not impose any reporting
requirements on you. However, your friend is required
to report all exchanges with state officials under
Government Code Section 86107(d).

     I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have
any further questions, please contact Ted Prim, a
member of the Commission staff.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman, for the
                                        Commission

DHL:jp

PETER H. BEHR
CLARK BLAIR-HALL
DOUGLAS BOSCO
GEORGE BUSCH MAN
DONALD GRUNSKY
MILTON MARKS
H.A. MILLEY

California Legislature

Request No. 75 1179
M.L. RICHARDSON
NEWTON R. RUSSELL
DIXCRAFT
KENNETH STRAHS
JOHN STULL
HOWARD WAY
JAMES E. WHITMORE

Date Resolved 9-22-75
Senate File 10-6-75
ROBERT B. RUSSELL

SENATE REPUBLICAN CAUCUS

DENNIS E. CARPENTER
        CHAIRMAN
STEVEN V. SEIZING
    CONSULTANT

111 S.N.P STREET SUITE C
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 443-9917

                                    September 13, 1975

Mr. Robert Stern
Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir:

This is to request an opinion on the possible effect of
Proposition 9 on a car pool I have been involved with,
involving myself and a registered lobbyist. The car pool
involves alternate day driving by each of us and is indi-
vidually equal in reciprocity. No money or other compen-
sation is exchanged between the other individual and myself.

My questions then are:

1.    Is such a car pool prohibited by Proposition 9,
      or by the regulations of the FPPC?

2.    If not prohibited, does such an arrangement
      require any form or report to the FPPC?

Your attention to this request for an opinion is appreciated
and a speedy reply will be of valuable assistance.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Bill Green, Jr.
                                        Senior Consultant
                                        Senate Republican Caucus


BG/nm
